title: -Trillium Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) 5-year Sole Source License and Operating Agreement

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a 5-year sole source
license and operating agreement for compressed natural gas with Trillium CNG for the
five-year period between FY2026 and FY2031, for a not-to-exceed amount of $250,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for the purchase of compressed natural gas (CNG) fuel is available in the
adopted FY2026 Equipment Maintenance Fund (Fund 672).
sections.current_situation:
California has approximately 304 public-access CNG stations, according to the U.S.
Department of Transportation. These stations primarily serve cars, light trucks, and
vans, with a smaller number also dispensing liquefied natural gas (LNG) for heavy-duty
vehicles. The stations are concentrated in metropolitan areas and along major
transportation corridors.
The City has been using CNG to power most of its waste collection fleet since
2012. While fuel usage and associated costs have declined as the number of CNG
vehicles in service has declined, there remains a broad community need for this
resource. The City, Berkeley Unified School District, City residents, and residents from
surrounding areas continue to rely upon CNG fuel. Since 1997, Trillium CNG has been
managing and operating the only CNG fueling station system within City limits under a
licensing agreement with the City. This licensing agreement expired June 30, 2024, and
was not renewed. Therefore, Council approval for this request is needed to prevent
disruption to the City’s supply of CNG for its fleet, public, and community partner
vehicles.

Trillium Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) 5-year Sole Source
License & Operating Agreement
CONSENT CALENDAR
July 29, 2025
Operation/Management Service for Natural Gas Vehicle Fueling Equipment to be
located at the City Transfer Station’s Clementina Site. Two agencies responded:
Pinnacle CNG Company and Trillium. Both applications were reviewed and evaluated
by City staff, and Trillium was found to be the most responsive and responsible bidder.
On June 9, 1998, the City of Berkeley entered into a ten-year license agreement with
Trillium to provide CNG fuel, and to build, operate and maintain a Compressed Natural
Gas Facility located adjacent to the City’s Transfer Station.
In 2008, the City extended the agreement an additional five years with a new expiration
date of December 2013.
In FY2010, the projected expenditures for CNG were anticipated to exceed the City
Manger’s authorized threshold of $100,000. Therefore, the City Council approved
Resolution 64,669-N.S. authorizing payment of $625,000 for the period FY 2010
through FY2014.
On September 13, 2016, by Council Resolution No. 67,662-N.S., the agreement was
increased by $1,000,000 from $888,274 to $1,888,724 and extended through June 30,
2019. By mutual agreement, the term of the Contract was extended through June 30,
2024. The agreement expired on June 30, 2024 and Trillium interpreted the contract to
be automatically extended. As a best practice, the City is executing a new sole source
contract to retain services, rather than continuing to remain on the expired contract.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Natural gas is the cleanest burning alternative transportation fuel ava
sections.rationale:
Trillium will continue managing and operating the City’s high volume CNG station. The
Department of Public Works currently has a limited number of vehicles that use CNG
fuel. However, while there are other CNG stations available in surrounding Bay Area
cities, this high-volume station is open to the public and several private local entities
(including Berkeley Unified School District) to fuel their vehicles.
sections.alternatives:
Closing the fueling station and ceasing to provide CNG on site at the Transfer Station is
an alternative action. This would leave City fleet vehicles, particularly those involved in
rubbish and recycling collection, Berkeley Unified School District, City residents and the
surrounding areas without a local CNG fueling station. Decommissioning the fueling

cost prohibitive.

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
TRILLIUM COMPRESSED NATURAL GAS (CNG) 5-YEAR SOLE SOURCE LICENSE
& OPERATING AGREEMENT
WHEREAS, on August 29, 1997, an invitation for bid was duly advertised, the City’s
Public Works staff evaluated all bids submitted; the bid was awarded to Trillium USA
(now Trillium CNG), determined to be the most qualified bidder; and
WHEREAS, on June 14, 2005, the Berkeley City Council adopted Resolution No.
62,943-N.S., authorizing the City Manager to increase the purchase order for
compressed natural gas (CNG) with Trillium CNG and authorizing expenditures not to
exceed $200,000 annually for Fiscal Years 2006 through 2008; and
WHEREAS, on October 27, 2009, the Berkeley City Council adopted Resolution No.
64,669-N.S., authorizing the City Manager to increase the purchase order for
compressed natural gas (CNG) with Trillium CNG and authorizing expenditures not to
exceed $625,000 for Fiscal Years 2010 through 2014; and
WHEREAS, on September 13, 2016, by Council Resolution No. 67,662-N.S., the
agreement was increased $1,000,000 from $888,274 to $1,888,724 and extended
through June 30, 2019; and
WHEREAS, by mutual agreement, the term of the Contract was extended through June
30, 2024, and expired at the end of the term and Trillium interpreted the contract to be
automatically extended; and
WHEREAS, the City will enter into a new contract with funds available in the Fiscal Year
2026 adopted budget in Equipment Maintenance Fund 672.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to enter into a five-year sole source license and operating
agreement for compressed natural gas with Trillium CNG for the five-year period Fiscal
Year 2026 through Fiscal Year 2031 for a not-to-exceed amount of $250,000.
